## 7. Target Services Mapping

# Current-to-Azure Service Mapping Table

| Current Service/Component      | Current Technology                | Target Azure Service                | Migration Approach         | Notes                                                                                   |
|-------------------------------|-----------------------------------|-------------------------------------|---------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Web Server                    | ASP.NET MVC, JavaScript, React.js | Azure App Service / AKS             | Refactor/Modernize        | Web/API endpoints moved to App Service or AKS for managed scalability and HA            |
| API Gateway                   | NGINX/Kong on RHEL VMs            | Azure API Management / App Gateway  | Rehost/Modernize          | Initial rehost as VM, then modernize to Azure API Management for advanced features       |
| Application Servers           | Node.js Microservices on K8s      | Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS)      | Modernize                 | Containerized workloads migrated to AKS for managed orchestration and scaling           |
| Database                      | SQL Server 2016 AlwaysOn Cluster  | Azure SQL Managed Instance          | Modernize                 | Managed HA/DR, geo-redundant backup, AD authentication, GDPR/SOX compliance             |
| Database (Oracle)             | OracleDB on VM                    | Oracle 19c on Azure (BaseDB/Exacs)  | Modernize                 | Production only; critical business data, geo-redundant backup                           |
| Cache                         | Redis on RHEL VMs                 | Azure Cache for Redis               | Modernize                 | Clustered for HA, managed patching, geo-redundant backup                                |
| Messaging                     | RabbitMQ/Kafka on RHEL VMs        | Azure Service Bus / Event Grid      | Modernize                 | Managed messaging for reliability, scalability, and integration                         |
| Load Balancer (External)      | F5 BIG-IP                         | Azure Application Gateway (WAF)     | Modernize                 | WAF enabled, HTTPS only, external and internal listeners                                |
| Load Balancer (Internal)      | Windows NLB                       | Azure Load Balancer                 | Modernize                 | Internal load balancing for AKS and App tier                                            |
| File Shares                   | SMB on Windows Server             | Azure Files / Azure NetApp Files    | Modernize                 | AD-integrated SMB shares, NFS for high performance, geo-redundant backup                |
| Monitoring                    | Prometheus, Grafana on Ubuntu     | Azure Monitor, Log Analytics        | Modernize                 | Unified metrics/logs, alerting, SIEM integration                                        |
| Logging                       | ELK Stack on Ubuntu               | Azure Log Analytics, Monitor        | Modernize                 | Centralized log aggregation, query, and alerting                                        |
| SIEM                          | Splunk                            | Microsoft Sentinel                  | Modernize                 | Security incident and event management, threat detection                                |
| Credential Management         | CyberArk                          | Azure Key Vault                     | Modernize                 | Secrets, keys, certificates, automated rotation, RBAC                                   |
| Identity Provider (Internal)  | Active Directory (LDAP/Kerberos)  | Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD)       | Integrate/Modernize       | SSO, RBAC, OAuth/OpenID Connect for internal/external users                             |
| Identity Provider (External)  | Azure AD                          | Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD)       | Integrate                 | SSO, API access, third-party integrations                                               |
| Batch Jobs                    | Scheduled Tasks, Windows/Linux    | Azure Function Apps / Logic Apps    | Modernize                 | Serverless batch/integration jobs, event-driven automation                              |
| Backup                        | Veeam, SAN-based                  | Azure Backup, Recovery Services Vault| Modernize                | Nightly full, 15-min log, geo-redundant, point-in-time restore                          |
| Disaster Recovery             | SAN Replication, DR Site          | Azure Site Recovery, Geo-Replication| Modernize                 | Annual DR testing, documented failover, RTO/RPO targets                                 |
| Network Security              | VLANs, ACLs, Firewalls            | Azure Firewall, NSGs, DDoS Protection| Modernize                | Segregated subnets, forced tunneling, threat intelligence, geo-blocking                 |
| Monitoring Dashboards         | RabbitMQ Dashboard, Grafana       | Azure Monitor, Log Analytics        | Modernize                 | Unified dashboard, alerting, and incident response                                      |
| Integration with Legacy Billing| Linked Server, File Share         | Azure Logic Apps, API Management    | Refactor                  | API-based integration, secure connectivity                                              |
| External Integrations         | PayPal API, POS, E-Commerce       | Azure API Management, Private Endpoints | Integrate              | Secure, monitored, and governed API access                                              |

---

**Notes:**
- Migration approaches are chosen based on modernization, operational efficiency, and risk mitigation.
- All data and services are deployed in West Europe to comply with GDPR and EU data residency requirements.
- Security, monitoring, and automation are enhanced using Azure-native services.
- Legacy OS and manual processes are targeted for modernization or re-platforming where feasible.
- All critical workloads are configured for HA/DR, with geo-redundant backup and annual DR testing.

This mapping ensures a secure, scalable, and compliant transition from on-premises to Azure, aligned with the intake assessment and migration strategy [see asr-report-50000.md] (blob) [see asr-report-50000.md] (blob) [see asr-report-50000.md] (blob).

## 8. Deployment Guidance

# Azure Migration Deployment Guidance

---

## 1) Prerequisites

| Requirement                | Details                                                                                  |
|----------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Azure Subscriptions        | At least one production subscription and one non-production (test/dev) subscription      |
| Permissions                | Owner or Contributor role on target subscriptions; User Access Administrator for RBAC    |
| Tools                      | Azure CLI, Azure PowerShell, Azure DevOps, Azure Migrate, Azure Storage Explorer         |
| Identity                   | Azure AD tenant configured; Managed Identities and Service Principals registered         |
| Network                    | ExpressRoute or VPN Gateway configured for hybrid connectivity (if required)             |
| Compliance                 | Data residency and access restrictions enforced (EU only, GDPR/SOX compliance)           |
| Security                   | Key Vault, Defender for Cloud, Firewall, WAF, DDoS Protection configured                 |
| Monitoring                 | Log Analytics Workspace, Application Insights, Azure Monitor, Action Groups provisioned  |
| Backup/DR                  | Azure Backup vaults, Recovery Services Vault, and DR plans in place                      |
| Documentation              | Operational support model (RACI), migration acceptance tests, and rollback plan prepared |
| Pre-Migration Tasks        | Inventory all dependencies, sanitize test/dev data, validate network access, schedule downtime windows, obtain change approvals, and communicate migration plan to stakeholders |

---

## 2) Deployment Sequence

1. **Provision Core Networking**
   - Deploy VNets, subnets, NSGs, route tables, and VNet peering for all environments.
   - Configure VPN Gateway/ExpressRoute if hybrid connectivity is required.

2. **Deploy Security and Identity Resources**
   - Set up Azure Key Vault, Managed Identities, Defender for Cloud, Azure Firewall, WAF policies, and DDoS Protection.
   - Integrate with Azure AD and configure RBAC assignments.

3. **Provision Data and Storage Resources**
   - Deploy Azure SQL Managed Instance, Azure Cache for Redis, Azure Files/NetApp Files, and Storage Accounts.
   - Configure Private Endpoints and enable geo-redundant backup.

4. **Deploy Compute Resources**
   - Deploy AKS clusters, App Services, Function Apps, and any required VMs (e.g., API Gateway nodes).
   - Integrate with Container Registry and configure scaling/HA.

5. **Configure Monitoring and Logging**
   - Set up Log Analytics Workspace, Application Insights, Azure Monitor, and Action Groups.
   - Enable diagnostic settings and alert rules for all resources.

6. **Deploy Application Code and Integrations**
   - Use CI/CD pipelines to deploy application containers, web/API code, and batch jobs.
   - Configure Logic Apps, Service Bus, and external API integrations.

7. **Data Migration**
   - Migrate databases using Azure Database Migration Service or native tools.
   - Migrate file shares and application data to Azure Files/NetApp Files.

8. **Validation and Testing**
   - Run migration acceptance tests, validate connectivity, security, and compliance.
   - Perform DR/backup validation and failover testing.

9. **Cutover and Go-Live**
   - Schedule and execute cutover during approved downtime window.
   - Monitor post-migration health, performance, and security.

10. **Decommission Legacy Infrastructure**
    - Decommission on-premises resources after successful migration and validation.

---

## 3) Infrastructure as Code (IaC) Recommendations

| Recommendation            | Details                                                                                   |
|---------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Tool                      | **Bicep** (preferred for Azure-native, modular, and readable IaC) or **Terraform** (if multi-cloud or advanced state management is required) |
| Module Structure          | Organize modules by resource type: networking, security, compute, data, monitoring, identity, and shared services. Use environment-specific parameter files. |
| Source Control            | Store all IaC code in Git (Azure Repos or GitHub), with feature branching and pull requests.|
| Pipeline Integration      | Integrate IaC deployment with Azure DevOps or GitHub Actions pipelines for automated, repeatable deployments. |
| Automation                | Use pipeline stages for validation (what-if), deployment, and post-deployment verification.|
| Secrets Management        | Reference secrets and sensitive parameters from Azure Key Vault in IaC templates.          |
| Compliance                | Enforce Azure Policy and Blueprints for governance and compliance as code.                |
| Documentation             | Document all modules, parameters, and deployment steps for operational support.           |

---

**Summary:**  
Follow a phased, automated, and policy-driven deployment approach using Bicep or Terraform, with all resources provisioned via IaC and deployed through CI/CD pipelines. Validate all configurations against security, compliance, and operational requirements before production cutover. Maintain rollback and acceptance test plans for migration assurance.

---

*References: Application_Design_Document.docx, Security_Controls.docx, DevOps_Practices.docx, DR_Plan.docx, Network_Design_Document.docx, Operational_Support_Model.docx* [see asr-report-50000.md] (blob) [see asr-report-50000.md] (blob) [see tables_snapshot_50000.jsonl] (blob) [see tables_snapshot_50000.jsonl] (blob) [see asr-report-50000.md] (blob)

## 9. Security and Compliance

# 1) Security Controls

| Control Category   | Control                                    | Implementation                                                                 | Azure Service                       |
|--------------------|--------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------|
| Network Security   | Segregated network zones                   | DMZ, App, DB, Monitoring, Management subnets with NSGs and ACLs               | Azure Virtual Network, NSG, Firewall|
| Network Security   | Perimeter protection                       | All external traffic via WAF, only HTTPS (443) allowed                        | Azure Application Gateway (WAF)     |
| Network Security   | Centralized ingress/egress control         | Forced tunneling, threat intelligence, geo-blocking                           | Azure Firewall, DDoS Protection     |
| Network Security   | Private endpoints for PaaS                 | Private Link for SQL MI, Redis, Storage, Key Vault                            | Private Endpoint, Private Link      |
| Data Protection    | Encryption at rest and in transit          | TDE for SQL, disk encryption, TLS 1.2/HTTPS for all traffic                   | Azure SQL MI, Storage, Key Vault    |
| Data Protection    | Backup and DR                              | Geo-redundant backup, annual DR testing, Recovery Services Vault               | Azure Backup, Recovery Services Vault|
| Data Protection    | Data classification and retention          | C3 (Confidential) in Prod, C2 (General) in Test/Dev, 7-year retention policy  | Azure Information Protection        |
| Identity Management| AD-integrated authentication               | Azure AD for SSO, OAuth/OpenID Connect, RBAC for all resources                | Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD)       |
| Identity Management| Managed identities and RBAC                | System-assigned identities for all compute/data, least-privilege RBAC         | Managed Identity, Azure RBAC        |
| Identity Management| Credential and secret management           | All secrets, keys, certificates in Key Vault, regular rotation                | Azure Key Vault                     |
| Monitoring         | Centralized logging and SIEM               | All logs/metrics to Log Analytics, SIEM integration for threat detection      | Azure Monitor, Log Analytics, Sentinel|
| Monitoring         | Real-time alerting and incident response   | Alert rules for critical events, routed to Action Groups and ITSM             | Azure Monitor, Action Groups        |
| Monitoring         | Regular vulnerability assessment           | Penetration testing, security patching, risk tracking                         | Defender for Cloud, Security Center |

---

# 2) Compliance Requirements

| Requirement                        | Standard         | Implementation Status      | Notes                                                                                   |
|-------------------------------------|------------------|---------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Data residency in EU                | GDPR             | Enforced                  | All data stored and accessed only within EU; no access from outside EU                  |
| Personal data processing            | GDPR             | In place                  | Handles account, authentication, financial, employment, security, user activity logs    |
| Auditability and data subject rights| GDPR             | In place                  | Automated data subject requests, audit logs, SIEM integration                           |
| Data retention (7 years)            | GDPR, SOX        | In place                  | Retention policy enforced for all regulated data                                        |
| Encryption at rest and in transit   | GDPR, SOX        | In place                  | TDE, disk encryption, TLS 1.2/HTTPS for all data flows                                  |
| Access control and RBAC             | GDPR, SOX        | In place                  | RBAC enforced for all resources, least-privilege principle                              |
| Security patching and vulnerability | GDPR, SOX        | Ongoing                   | Regular patching, penetration testing, risk tracking                                    |
| Official-sensitive information      | Internal Policy  | In place                  | Customer IPs, network diagrams, risk assessments handled per internal security policies |
| Local EU data protection laws       | Local/EU         | Enforced                  | Additional local requirements applied as needed                                         |
| Privacy and security assessment     | Internal Policy  | In place                  | Assessment ID: VSR-2023-015; risks tracked and mitigated                                |
| External/internal user access       | GDPR, SOX        | In place                  | Both internal and external users supported; access controls and monitoring enforced     |

---

**Summary:**
- Security controls are implemented at multiple layers: network, data, identity, and monitoring, using Azure-native services.
- Compliance with GDPR, SOX, and local EU data protection laws is strictly enforced, including data residency, encryption, retention, and auditability.
- Risks such as unauthorized access and the need for regular patching are tracked and mitigated through ongoing assessments and operational controls.
- All controls and requirements are documented in the Security_Controls.docx and referenced in the privacy/security assessment (Assessment ID: VSR-2023-015) [see asr-report-50000.md] (blob) [see asr-report-50000.md] (blob) [see asr-report-50000.md] (blob) [see tables_snapshot_50000.jsonl] (blob).

## 10. Well-Architected Framework Alignment

# Azure Well-Architected Framework Alignment

| Pillar                | Design Element                                      | Implementation                                                                                                   | Score (1-5) |
|-----------------------|-----------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------|
| Cost Optimization     | Right-sizing, automation, reserved instances        | AKS node pools, SQL MI vCores, App Service plans sized per environment; automation via CI/CD and IaC; LRS/GRS for storage; cost monitoring in place | 4           |
| Operational Excellence| CI/CD, monitoring, automation, runbooks             | Azure DevOps pipelines for phased rollout, ARM/Bicep for IaC, Azure Monitor, Log Analytics, documented RACI, migration acceptance tests, rollback plans | 5           |
| Performance Efficiency| Autoscaling, managed services, caching              | AKS with autoscaling, Azure SQL MI with HA, Azure Cache for Redis, Service Bus, App Gateway, App Insights for telemetry | 5           |
| Reliability           | HA/DR, geo-redundant backup, failover testing       | SQL MI multi-zone HA, geo-replication, AKS node pools, clustered Redis, annual DR testing, Azure Backup, Recovery Services Vault | 5           |
| Security              | Segregated networks, RBAC, encryption, SIEM, compliance | NSGs, Azure Firewall, WAF, DDoS Protection, Key Vault, Defender for Cloud, Sentinel, GDPR/SOX enforced, data residency, regular patching, SIEM integration | 5           |

---

## Pillar Details & Examples

- **Cost Optimization:**  
  - All compute, database, and storage resources are sized per environment (prod, test, dev) and use automation for deployment and scaling. Storage redundancy (LRS/GRS) is chosen based on data criticality. Azure Monitor is used for cost tracking and optimization recommendations.  
  - *Score: 4* (Continuous review and reserved instance adoption can further improve cost efficiency) [see asr-report-50000.md] (blob).

- **Operational Excellence:**  
  - CI/CD pipelines (Azure DevOps) automate build, test, and deployment. Infrastructure is provisioned via ARM/Bicep, with rollback and acceptance test plans. Monitoring and alerting are centralized, and operational support is documented in a RACI matrix.  
  - *Score: 5* (Best practices fully implemented for automation and operations) [see asr-report-50000.md] (blob).

- **Performance Efficiency:**  
  - AKS supports horizontal pod autoscaling, SQL MI and Redis are deployed in HA/clustered configurations, and App Gateway provides scalable ingress. Application Insights and Log Analytics provide real-time telemetry for tuning.  
  - *Score: 5* (Design supports dynamic scaling and high throughput) [see asr-report-50000.md] (blob).

- **Reliability:**  
  - Multi-zone HA for SQL MI, AKS, and Redis; geo-redundant backups; annual DR testing; and documented failover procedures. Azure Site Recovery is used for any VM-based workloads.  
  - *Score: 5* (Comprehensive HA/DR and backup strategy) [see asr-report-50000.md] (blob).

- **Security:**  
  - Segregated VNets/subnets, NSGs, Azure Firewall, WAF, DDoS Protection, Key Vault for secrets, Defender for Cloud, Sentinel for SIEM, and strict GDPR/SOX compliance. Data is encrypted at rest and in transit, and RBAC is enforced for all resources.  
  - *Score: 5* (Multi-layered security, compliance, and monitoring) [see asr-report-50000.md] (blob).

---

**Summary:**  
The proposed Azure design for ERMS demonstrates strong alignment with all five pillars of the Azure Well-Architected Framework, with particular strengths in operational excellence, reliability, performance, and security. Cost optimization is well addressed, with opportunities for further improvement through ongoing cost reviews and reserved instance adoption.

---

*References: Application_Design_Document.docx, Security_Controls.docx, DR_Plan.docx, Operational_Support_Model.docx, Cloud_Migration_Assessment.xlsx*

## 11. Appendix

# Appendix

---

## 1) Assumptions

- All Azure resources are deployed in West Europe to enforce EU data residency and GDPR compliance.
- Database sizing, vCore counts, and node pools are based on current environment data and expected growth.
- App Service and AKS node counts are sized for high availability and environment needs.
- All secrets and credentials are managed in Azure Key Vault, replacing CyberArk.
- All integrations (e.g., PayPal API, legacy billing) are compatible with Azure networking and security models.
- Non-production environments are refreshed from sanitized production backups to avoid sensitive data exposure.
- All technical documentation and operational support models are updated prior to migration.
- Vendor support is not available; all development and maintenance are in-house.
- Migration cutover will be scheduled during approved downtime windows.
- All resiliency configurations (HA/DR, backup) are tested and validated as part of migration acceptance criteria.
- Pricing and cost estimates are based on typical Azure retail rates and may vary; actual costs should be validated with the Azure Calculator.
- All compliance requirements (GDPR, SOX, local EU laws) are enforced through Azure Policy and operational controls.
- Legacy OS dependencies will be addressed via upgrade or containerization as part of migration.

---

## 2) Dependencies

- **Internal Dependencies:**
  - Web Layer: ASP.NET MVC, JavaScript frontend
  - API Gateway: RHEL 8.6 (API-GW-01/02), secure API traffic, routing, authentication
  - Application Layer: Node.js microservices on Kubernetes
  - Data Layer: SQL Server 2016 AlwaysOn cluster, AD integrated authentication
  - Batch/Job Processing: Nightly data sync, report generation, file share dependencies
  - Monitoring & Logging: Prometheus, Grafana, ELK Stack, Splunk, Application Insights
  - Credential Management: CyberArk (to be replaced by Azure Key Vault)
  - Load Balancers: F5 (external), Windows NLB (internal)
  - AD Groups: ERMS_Users, ERMS_Admins

- **External Dependencies:**
  - Legacy billing system (linked server integration)
  - PayPal API (payment gateway)
  - Azure AD (external identity provider)
  - POS system and E-Commerce frontend (API integrations)
  - Internet connectivity for user access and outbound API calls

- **Prerequisites:**
  - Azure subscriptions and permissions
  - ExpressRoute or VPN Gateway for hybrid connectivity (if required)
  - Azure AD tenant and RBAC assignments
  - Azure Backup vaults, Recovery Services Vault, DR plans
  - Log Analytics Workspace, Application Insights, Azure Monitor, Action Groups
  - Updated technical documentation and migration acceptance tests

---

## 3) Risks and Mitigations

| Risk                                             | Impact                                   | Probability | Mitigation Strategy                                                                                  |
|--------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------|-------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Poor/incomplete technical documentation          | Migration delays, errors, rework         | Medium      | Update and complete documentation before migration; knowledge transfer sessions with SMEs            |
| Legacy OS dependencies                           | Migration failure, security risk         | Medium      | Assess legacy components, plan for OS upgrades or containerization                                   |
| In-house developed application, no vendor support| Limited expert help, slower issue resolution | Medium      | Ensure internal SMEs are available, document knowledge transfer                                      |
| Single-instance database architecture            | Scalability and resilience limitations   | Medium      | Refactor for cloud-native multi-instance or managed DB                                               |
| Manual credential/key management                 | Integration issues, security risk        | Medium      | Migrate to Azure Key Vault, automate credential rotation                                             |
| Linked server integration with legacy billing    | Integration failure post-migration       | Medium      | Map dependencies, test integration in cloud, consider API-based integration                          |
| Scheduled jobs and file share dependencies       | Job failures, data loss                  | Medium      | Inventory jobs, migrate to cloud-native scheduling and storage                                       |
| Release management constraints                   | Delayed cutover, rollback issues         | Medium      | Automate approvals, optimize downtime windows, use blue/green deployment                             |
| Unauthorized access to personal data             | Regulatory fines, reputational damage    | Low         | Enforce RBAC, regular security reviews, SIEM monitoring, MFA                                         |
| Regular security patching required               | Increased vulnerability                  | Medium      | Implement automated patching, vulnerability scanning, and regular reviews                            |
| Migration downtime exceeds window                | Business disruption                      | Low         | Detailed migration plan, phased rollout, rollback procedures                                         |
| Data loss during migration                       | Regulatory and business impact           | Low         | Comprehensive backup, test restores, DR drills                                                       |
| Integration incompatibility (external APIs)      | Service disruption                       | Low         | Early integration testing, fallback plans, API versioning                                            |
| Cost overruns                                    | Budget impact                            | Low         | Use Azure Cost Management, monitor usage, right-size resources, review reserved instance options      |
